fact: September 28 sits exactly one week after the autumn equinox, which means day and night are still almost perfectly equal in length!
In the Northern Hemisphere, leaves are exploding with color right now - maple trees turn bright red while oak trees go golden yellow!
Monarch butterflies are typically in the middle of their epic 3,000-mile journey south to Mexico, and millions of them are often flying overhead right now!
This date falls in the middle of Libra season, making it one of the most 'balanced' days of the year according to astrology!
September 28 is day 271 of the year, which means there are exactly 94 days left until New Year's Eve!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, spring is in full swing and baby animals are being born everywhere!
The sun often sets with extra-bright orange and pink colors around this time because autumn air is clearer and crisper than summer air!
Squirrels are going absolutely nuts right now in northern climates - they're frantically burying acorns and can hide up to 10,000 nuts before winter!
Apple trees are typically at their peak ripeness in many regions, making this prime apple-picking season!
September 28 marks the time when spiders often start moving indoors to escape the cold, which is why you might see more eight-legged visitors in your house!
The moon often looks extra huge and orange when it rises around this date because it's closer to the horizon during autumn evenings!
In northern regions, the first frost can happen any day now, turning spider webs into glittering crystal decorations!
